description: A signal handler calls non-async-signal-safe functions or loses signals, deadlocking or skipping cleanup.
body: Failure mode: a handler for SIGTERM/SIGCHLD calls malloc, printf, or takes a lock, which is unsafe because the handler interrupts arbitrary code and can re-enter a non-reentrant function and deadlock or corrupt state; or it assumes each signal queues, missing collapsed SIGCHLD and leaving zombies. Detection signals: the process hangs during shutdown or under signal load; defunct/zombie children accumulate; intermittent corruption tied to signal timing. Do not do real work inside a handler. Safer fallback: set a sig_atomic_t flag or write to a self-pipe and handle work in the main loop, reap children in a waitpid WNOHANG loop, and verify clean shutdown under repeated signals.
